title: Alpha Engine apply_audit artifact (eval-storage contract, v1)
description: FROZEN cross-repo contract for config/apply_audit/{date}.json (+ latest.json mirror) — produced by crucible-backtester optimizer/apply_audit.py at the end of every evaluate optimizer stage, consumed by crucible-evaluator (grades RED when a loop has been blocked N consecutive weeks with no human ack). One record per auto-apply loop, emitted REGARDLESS of outcome, so a blocked/starved/broken apply loop is impossible to miss (config#1841). Lifted from crucible-backtester contracts/apply_audit.schema.json into nousergon_lib.contracts on the second-adoption signal (config#1861); producer and consumer now validate against this single artifact. Additive-only evolution: consumers MUST tolerate unknown extra fields; renames/removals (including removing a blocked_by slug) require a schema_version bump coordinated with the consumer. Adding NEW blocked_by slugs or outcome-irrelevant fields is additive.

as_of.description: Evaluator run date (trading-day normalized) — matches the dated S3 key.

generated_at.description: UTC ISO-8601 wall-clock write time (two-axis date doctrine: as_of is the trading day, this is the session timestamp).

outcome.description: promoted = live config written this run (for executor_params under assembler cutover: the assembler wrote the live key). blocked = a recommendation existed but a named guardrail refused it. insufficient_data = inputs too thin to form a recommendation (honest starvation, not a guardrail). error = the loop (or the stage before it) raised — the failure ALSO surfaces via completeness/failed_modules; this record is the durable trace. disabled = live write short-circuited by an operator flag (--freeze, shadow mode / enforce flag off).

blocked_by.description: Stable slugs naming the guardrail(s) that refused the promotion, derived from the real gate code: oos_degradation (weight_optimizer._validate_oos), confidence_below_medium (weight n_samples bar), max_single_change / min_meaningful_change (weight_optimizer.apply_weights; min_meaningful_change also covers research_optimizer status no_improvement), significance_floor (config#1426 enforce_significance — weight canonical signed-IC floor / veto Wilson-LB block), min_lift_over_base_rate (veto insufficient_lift), precision_ci_below_base_rate (veto insufficient_confidence), min_threshold_change (veto apply proximity gate), alpha_floor / min_trades_to_promote / negative_rank_metric / baseline_magnitude_floor / min_improvement / min_psr (executor_optimizer.recommend gates), assembler_skip (cutover assembler saw no promotable artifact), unclassified_guardrail (safety valve: an apply() rejection the producer classifier does not know — a producer bug to fix, surfaced loudly instead of mis-binned). null whenever outcome != blocked.

consecutive_blocked_weeks.description: Carry-forward counter read from the PRIOR latest.json: +1 on blocked (absent prior artifact => 1), reset to 0 on promoted or insufficient_data, carried unchanged on error/disabled (neither evidence of blocking nor unblocking). Single-owner artifact — only apply_audit.py writes this prefix, so the read-then-write is race-free by ownership.

detail.description: Human-readable one-liner: the guardrail rejection reason / starvation note / error text.

proposed.description: The recommendation the guardrails evaluated (suggested_weights / recommended_params / {veto_confidence}); null when no recommendation was formed.

current.description: The baseline the proposal was measured against (current_weights / baseline_params / {veto_confidence}); null when unavailable.
